config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think individual rights should be the default priority because once society is allowed to override them too easily, it can become an excuse for abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment of minorities. Society is important, but it is strongest when it protects personal freedom, dignity, and due process rather than treating people as tools for the common good. From my perspective, the group should have limits on its power over the individual, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman dan nilai yang kuat di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat hak individu sebagai batas utama terhadap kekuasaan mayoritas, karena tanpa batas itu, “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau penyalahgunaan wewenang. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati kebebasan, martabat, dan proses hukum setiap orang. Jadi bagi saya, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang tetap menempatkan hak dasar sebagai fondasi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to pressure dissent, excuse corruption, or silence minorities, so I do not trust society as a blanket justification for overriding personal rights. At the same time, I do not think individual rights mean selfishness; people also have responsibilities to others, and public safety or public health can justify some limits if they are narrow, fair, and accountable. So the real principle should be that society can set limits only when it has a strong reason and clear due process, not because the majority simply wants its way.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika Serikat saya melihat hak individu justru sebagai pagar utama terhadap kekuasaan mayoritas. Argumen “demi kepentingan masyarakat” memang kadang sah untuk keselamatan publik atau kesehatan umum, tetapi terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau perlakuan sewenang-wenang terhadap kelompok kecil. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang bisa membatasi diri, memberi alasan yang kuat, dan tetap patuh pada proses hukum. Jadi, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak dasar orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be placed above individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used too easily to justify pressure on dissent, discrimination against minorities, or abuse by people in power, so I do not trust that claim without strict limits. Public safety and health can justify some restrictions, but only if they are narrow, transparent, and accountable through due process. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices individuals whenever it is convenient, but one that protects both the common good and the basic dignity of each person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kita justru menunjukkan bahwa mayoritas sering bisa salah, dan hak individu diperlukan sebagai pagar agar negara atau opini publik tidak semena-mena. Memang ada keadaan seperti keselamatan publik atau kesehatan umum yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji lewat proses hukum. Jadi, bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi hak dasar sambil mengejar kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
